entities.name: Egyptians
entities.description: A group referred to as practicing 'fast or loose' (sleight of hand) and 'sortilegie' (sorcery), accused of using tricks to mimic magic.

entities.name: Eleazar
entities.card_name: historical figure cited in a trick description
entities.description: A person whose feat of making a pot fall from a cupboard via 'words' is described as a miracle, though explained here as a trick with black thread.

attributes.associated_feat: making a pot fall by words

entities.name: Steeven Tailor
entities.description: A person involved in a scheme to steal horses using deception and confederates.

entities.name: Josephus
entities.description: A historical source cited in relation to the feat of Eleazar.

entities.name: trickery with cards
entities.description: Various card tricks involving shuffling, keeping a 'kept' card, and transforming aces into knaves.

entities.name: juggling boxes
entities.description: The use of boxes with false bottoms or specialized shapes to transform grains, spices, or even a toad.

entities.name: sortilegie
entities.description: A term used to the practice of sorcery or magic, which Scot is debunking as trickery.
